mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 9.0% and continues to increase, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates a supply-demand imbalance where manufacturing outpaces residential investment, historically preceding equity drawdowns due to inventory correction risks.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between divergence and convergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising RSAFS indicates household liquidity hoarding that reduces equity market participation, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation periods do not show a statistically significant negative correlation with RSAFS growth (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales corporate earnings expectations, producing a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in contraction quarters are not significantly lower than expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 new jobs per month while unemployment remains below 8.0%, it signals a cyclical peak in labor demand that typically precedes equity drawdowns as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the decelerating labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in industrial production and federal funds rate indicates an overheating supply side met with tightening liquidity, which historically compresses equity valuations by raising discount rates faster than cash flows adjust.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between joint rise and joint fall regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rising RSAFS combined with stable PAYEMS indicates pent-up consumer demand being released into financial assets rather than goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as liquidity flows into equities.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery (UNRATE declining for 3 consecutive months)
failure_condition: Forward returns in post-shock recovery periods do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth (p > 0.05).
